title: Understanding the Range Formula
text: To find the range of a projectile, you can use the formula: \( R $$=$$ \frac{v**2 \sin(2\theta)}{g} \), where \( v \) is the initial velocity, \( $$\theta$$ \) is the angle of projection, and \( g \) is the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Substitute the Known Values
text: Given that the initial velocity \( v $$=$$ $$15$$ \) $$\frac{m}{s}$$, the angle \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ $$45$$ \) degrees, and the acceleration due to gravity on Mars \( g $$=$$ $$3.7$$ \) m/s², substitute these values into the range formula.

title: Calculate the Range
text: Using the formula \( R $$=$$ \frac{v**2 \sin(2\theta)}{g} \), calculate the range of the soccer kick.
